feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation free plan screen
entry.content:
Hi Learners,

Why the Free tier SAP Build process Automation is showing like this? I was doing Develop and automate with SAP Build course and doing this section Automating Processes with SAP Build Automation.

Regards

Kunal Bisht

entry.title: Unable to format SAP build output coming from LLM
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on an copilot created using SAP Build apps which talks with SAP AI core instance to answer queries asked .

The app works perfectly fine but the outcome lacks formats and colors which we usually see when we ask similiar questions to other copilots or chatbots.

Please find attached my apps output and other copilots output .Can you help me how I can retain the format of the outcome of LLM and display it on my application?

SAP Build 

SAP AI Core

entry.title: Actions in SAP Build Process Automation -- Need to call $Batch in Action Task
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to implement a batch call in Build Process Automation using an Action Task. I have configured the destination in the BTP Cockpit and also created an Action Project in SAP Build.

When testing the Action Project independently, I am able to retrieve the expected data. However, when using it within a workflow, I am encountering the following error:

Action Identifier:
post_batch
Destination:
GET_DATA
Error Message:
Action '7a1f2496-6c0d-48ce-a724-9ca2651d8fab' failed with the following error:
'Action metadata not found in database for tenant f5a191b7-809f-4113-87fe-6b21da19d542, project us10.93112af8trial.getdatacall, version 1.0.3, action group b315b59d-bafd-3cbf-a4a2-79a0743dfac0, and action post_batch'.

Log ID:
fdc268b4-2c5c-439a-814b-802b67e-us10

I have also attached a screenshot of the configuration.
Please let me know how I can resolve this issue.

Destination Config Screenshot:

Action Config Screenshot:

 

BPA workflow Screenshot:

Thanks,

Parth

entry.title: DME file format HDFC bank
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

I am configuring DME for XXX bank, India.

Bank has asked for certain fields

like

1.  record identifier - "~"

2.payment run date

3. Identification

4. payment document number

5. amount

6. Payment method

and below that for payment advice it needed

1. advice header - "~H"

2. Payment document number

3. Detail identifier - "~D"

4.Invoice number

5. Invoice amount

With the help of DME i have configured every single data required by the bank

But they require data of payment advice once the data of all the payment document is completed

to explain a bit more I will take e.g. of a pay run

which creates two payment document 200000393 & 200000394

so the output they require is like this

1. reference" ~ "

2.Run ID - 05.04.2013

3. Identification - test

4. Payment document - 200000393 ( it is paid after clearing two invoices 0100000014 )

5. Amount -970

6. payment method - N

1. reference" ~ "

2.Run ID - 05.04.2013

3. Identification - test

4. Payment document - 200000394 ( it is paid after clearing invoices 0100000016 )

5. Amount -975

6. payment method - N

1. ~H

2.Payment document - 200000393

3.~D

4. Invoice number-0100000014

5. Invoice amt - 970

1. ~H

2.Payment document - 200000394

3.~D

4. Invoice number-0100000016

5. Invoice amt - 975

Means all the payment advice data comes after all the part 1 data is finished

for this i have created two separate segments

part1 data is caputred in level1

and payment advice in level 2

so to enable this same I am trying to use delay output in DME segment but dont know how to use it exactly

Pls guide me how can we achieve the same

for your reference I am attaching you the format shared by bank along with sample data shared by the bank

we are on SAP R/3 4.7

entry.title: How to Generate a Dynamic URL for Approval Task in SAP Business Process Automation (SAP BPA)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm working on a workflow using SAP Build Process Automation and need help with generating a dynamic task approval link that can be sent via email notification to the approver.

Objective:

  • Generate a dynamic URL for each task instance.

  • Send the URL via email template or script task.

  • Clicking the link should open the approval task directly in My Inbox or SAP Build Work Zone / Launchpad.

Thanks in advance,
Parshuram

entry.title: User does not have sufficient privileges in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi

I have created a process automation like a bellow example 
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-salesorder.html

here it is working fine, and workflow is triggering while creating the instance from Process and Workflow>> Start New instance and Close. 

But when i created the Build App for the API like bellow:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

But here it is giving error at the time for workflow instance 

"responseText": "{\"error\":{\"code\":\"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.user.not.sufficient.privileges\",\"message\":\"User does not have sufficient privileges.\"}}"

I have assign the role also, But still giving error. Can any one help me please....

entry.title: SAP Build Action: Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
entry.content:
I'm trying to test an Action in SAP Build. However, when trying to select a Destination, I get the the following error:
 
Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
This error could happen for the following reasons:
Correlation ID: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
 
This is happening for all Actions.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Why is SAP Desktop Agent required for all automations?
entry.content:
I'm looking at putting together a SAP Build Process Automation project using only cloud services, so no local file storage or Excel / Outlook SDK.  In other words I'm looking to remove the SAP Desktop Agent dependancy.

However, on running the main process, the automaton is never called because it appears SAP Desktop Agent is a prerequisite for any automation, regardless of the dependencies (I'm using the CORE SDK only).

Can anyone confirm the above? Or is there a way of setting up an SAP Build Process Automation without the need for SAP Desktop Agent?

entry.title: Anyone Successfully Using SAP Desktop Agent on Mac?
entry.content: Can anyone confirm if the SAP Desktop Agent is available for Mac and whether it works properly? I remember there were some issues with installing it on Mac earlier. If anyone is currently using it on a Mac, could you please share your experience—specifically, whether it's functioning smoothly or not?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Can an automation be ran without the SAP Desktop Agent?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working an SAP Build Process Automation pilot project and have reached a point where the team is ready to start looking at deployment options in terms of how the automation will be ran. It seems like the desktop agent is necessary to run a scheduled unattended trigger. Ultimately, we want to avoid having to setup a virtual machine to carry out this particular automation. The below image contains our automation process and the options we see available. What would be the best way to run this particular scenario without the Desktop Agent? The automation currently uses the Outlook SDK, but research has led us to find that if we use Microsoft 365 SDK instead, we can run this scenario without the agent. Are these findings valid? If not, I would appreciate feedback on the best approach to help us move forward!

 

 

Regards,

Patrick Pienkowski

entry.title: Tutorials no longer open
entry.content: No matter which browser, the individual tutorials no longer open.

entry.title: Instalación de Desktop Agent en SBPA.
entry.content:
Estimados.

Buenas tardes.

Junto con saludar, quería solicitar ayuda, ya que actualmente tenemos una instalación del desktop agent de Sap Build Process Automation, para lo cual lo utilizamos para la función de las automatizaciones del Workflow implementado, para la instalación del ejecutable y servicio me guie por la siguiente documentación:

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/install-desktop-agent-3-8c42e11b33824acc8273713f276589ea
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/desktop-agent-service

En su momento se instaló el agente en el servidor a través de un usuario en específico, por lo cual, cuando el servidor realizaba las actualizaciones el agente se desconectaba debido a que solicitaba al usuario del servidor el ingreso de sesión, por dicho motivo, se realizó el cambio y se instaló en la máquina con el usuario de administrador, sin embargo, en la actualidad, cuando se realizan las actualizaciones automáticas del servidor también el agente se desconecta, dicho agente, ya se encuentra instalado también como servicio y dicho servicio cuando se visualiza el estado se encuentra en "running" , sin embargo, el agente aun así se desconecta. Esto me ocurre para el servidor de QA que tiene las actualizaciones de manera automática, no así, para el agente que se encuentra instalado en el servidor de PRD, ya que este servidor se encuentra con las actualizaciones programadas y el agente no se desconecta.

La versión que se está utilizando es la 3.32.58 y el modo se encuentra como "no asistido", si bien se entiende que existe una última actualización de dicho agente, no es compatible con la versión 1.48.34 del SDK ya deployado del proyecto. 

Quisiera saber si alguien ha instalado dicho agente en el servidor y como se puede estabilizar el estado de este.

Muchas gracias.

entry.title: How to Skip One Branch After the Other Completes in SAP Build Process Automation?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm building a process in SAP Build Process Automation and need help designing the following logic:

  • The process starts with an API trigger.

  • Then, I want to run two parallel branches:

    1. Show an approval form to a user (Process A).

    2. Wait for another API event (Process B).

My goal is:
As soon as either Process A or Process B completes, the other one should be automatically skipped. Only one branch should proceed based on whichever finishes first.

Is there a best practice or pattern to achieve this in SAP Build?
I've tried using branches and flags, but I'm unsure how to control the flow correctly to cancel/skip the other process.

Any guidance or examples would be really appreciated!

entry.title: To enable Studio Home Application in SAP Buildworkzone
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have followed the prerequisites in the below help documentation to configure the Studio Home Application in SAP Buildworkzone.
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-studio-home-application?q=studio+home

However, I could not see the studio home application in the Buildworkzone. I have refreshed the HTML content manager. I don't see such application available in workzone, but the other apps like My Inbox, Visibility scenario, Process etc.
I also read the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/empowering-business-to-manage-decisions-in-sap-build-process-automation/ba-p/14092130

Can someone help me to figure out what I was missing? or please chare clear steps to see the Studio Home application in Buildworkzone.

entry.title: Critical: SAP Desktop Agent Randomly Disconnecting in VM – Impacting Automation Execution
entry.content:
The SAP Desktop Agent in the VM is getting disconnected automatically at random intervals, with no consistent pattern. As a result, the automation stops unexpectedly, and users are left waiting for results that won’t be generated unless someone manually logs into the VM. This is impacting the reliability of the process and needs attention urgently.

I've attached two images to illustrate the issue:

  • The first image shows the agent in a disconnected state when no one is logged into the VM.

 

  • The second image shows that once I manually log in, the agent status changes to "Disconnected->Preparing", and only then does the automation start working.

entry.title: [SAP Build Process Automation] Desktop Agent does not synchronize deployed projects and stuck logs
entry.content:
Dear SAP Build Process Automation Community:

I'm having a persistent and very frustrating issue with my SAP Build Process Automation desktop agent. It doesn't seem to sync deployed projects despite seemingly correct configuration and several troubleshooting attempts. I would greatly appreciate any help, suggestions, or diagnostics based on your experiences.SAP Build Process Automation 

 

The core problem (with the desktop agent!):

My Desktop Agent (v3.37.57 on Windows 11) constantly displays the message "It appears no projects have been received yet" in the "Projects" window. Most worryingly, its local logs ("History/Events") are completely blocked, showing the last activity as [Exact date of capture, e.g., June 9, 2025], even though it is [Current date, e.g., June 18, 2025].

This indicates that the agent is not processing or updating its status locally, nor is it downloading projects that have been deployed.

 

Project context and configuration (Everything looks fine in the cloud!):

I've set up a project to automate the mass update of fixed asset master records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The cloud configuration appears to be correct:

Project: Mass Update Asset Master Data (57O).
Cloud Status: Deployed and started in RPADeveloper environment (confirmed in "Environments" > "Projects").
Automation Launcher: The Mass Update Asset Master Data (57O) artifact (Automation Launcher type) is configured with the main automation (MassUpdateAssetMasterData) in the "Start Manually from Agent" section.
Agent Connection to Cloud:
In the BTP lobby (Environments > RPADeveloper > Agent Management), my agent (NDTSCZ1166) appears as "Inactive" and "Connected".
In the agent's local settings (Configuration > Tenants), the connection to efdadad3trial.us10.process-automation.build.cloud.sap is "Active".

entry.title: Unable to add Northwind destination in SAP BUILD Apps integration
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I have a use case wherein I wish to create a Northwind destination in cockpit and add that destination as SAP system in SAP BUILD Apps.

 

Below are my destination details:

I am able to connect successfully from cockpit.

However, When I try to add this SAP system through Integrations tab , I get below error:

On Inspect, below are the details.

Please provide your valuable thoughts on this issue.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Add a Document Template Error
entry.content:
I am trying to create an artifact for a Document Template in SAP Build on BTP Trial. However, when I select the option to create a Document Template, I receive the following error:

"SAP_IPA_28014: The requested Document Information Extraction service operation could not be completed."

This error is preventing the creation of the template.

I have already assigned the role 'Document_Information_Extraction_UI_Templates_Admin' to my account.

Could you please help resolve this issue?

SAP Build 
SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: How to Handle Script Task Limit Exceeded Error in process Automation for Email Body Generation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on an automation in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) where we need to fetch data from an API and include it in the body of an email. Without the use of Agent.
Here’s the scenario:

  • We are using an HTTP action to call the API and retrieve the data.
  • A script task is used to convert the API response (which contains a responseArray with multiple EDIDC and EDID4 objects) into an HTML table to be used as the email body.
  • The script generates the HTML content and stores it in the context variable $.context.htmlTable.

However, we are facing an issue because the data size exceeds the 100 KB limit for the script task in BPA, 
"Script Task" failed
Error message: Execution of task 'script_1' has been stopped while executing the statement [<...], as it reached the time limit

. The API response can contain a large number of records, and converting this into HTML is causing the script to go beyond the allowed limit.

Here is the script we are using

// Step 1: Get the API response
var responseArray = $.context.action_get_getZidocZidoc_1.result.responseArray;
 
// Step 2: Validate response
if (!responseArray || responseArray.length === 0) {
throw new Error("No records found");
}
 
// Step 3: Escape HTML
function escapeHtml(text) {
text = (text === null || text === undefined) ? "" : String(text);
return text.replace(/&/g, "&").replace(/</g, "<").replace(/>/g, ">");
}
 
// Step 4: Build table rows
var rows = "";
for (var i = 0; i < responseArray.length; i++) {
var edidc = responseArray[i]["EDIDC"] || {};
rows += "<tr>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["DOCNUM"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["IDOCTP"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["MESTYP"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["CREDAT"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["SNDPRN"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["RCVPRN"]) + "</td>" +
"<td>" + escapeHtml(edidc["STATUS"]) + "</td>" +
"</tr>";
}
 
// Step 5: Final table
var html = "<h3>IDOC Report</h3>" +
"<table border='1' cellpadding='5' cellspacing='0'>" +
"<tr><th>DOCNUM</th><th>IDOCTP</th><th>MESTYP</th><th>CREDAT</th><th>SNDPRN</th><th>RCVPRN</th><th>STATUS</th></tr>" +
rows +
"</table>";
 
// Step 6: Store in context
$.context.htmlTable = html;



Could you please suggest a solution or workaround to handle this limit exceeded error? Some ideas we are considering include:

  • Splitting the data into smaller chunks and processing them in multiple script tasks.
  • Using an external service to generate the HTML and call it via an action.
  • Any other best practices or standard approaches in BPA for handling large datasets in script tasks.

    Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: What's the future of SAP technical consultant?
entry.content:
With the introduction of next-generation technologies like SAP BTP, ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), SAP Build (LCNC tools), and SAP Joule (AI), it's clear that SAP is moving towards a clean core, cloud-based future.

However, in reality, many organizations are still operating on legacy ABAP systems, and real-world adoption of these new tools remains limited. With the end of ECC support by 2027, this gap raises some concerns.

❓Key Questions:

1. What is the realistic future for SAP technical consultants in this evolving landscape?

2. Is it practical to invest time in RAP, BTP, or AI tools, given the current slow adoption?

3. Are any real projects or clients actively implementing these technologies?

Would appreciate insights from those currently working on RAP or BTP-based projects or involved in modern SAP implementations.

Thank you!
